title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 19.
content:
There was an Old Person of Leeds,
    Whose head was infested with beads;
    She sat on a stool,
    And ate gooseberry fool,
    Which agreed with that person of Leeds.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 19.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
